raw.objective: “to develop a population pharmacokinetic model for VPA based on protein-binding saturation in pediatric patients with epilepsy.”
raw.covariates: Tested: SEX, AGE, WT, Formulation (Syrup, Conventional tablet or SR tablet), comedication. Included: CBZ, WT, AGE, TDD
raw.observations: Model supports age-dependent PK reflecting maturation of UGR enzymes in children. Dose-dependent maximum effect (DDE) model best fitted for aim of the study. The non-linear correlation between DDVPA and CL can be due to concentration-dependent protein binding of VPA. A nonlinear effect of TDM and protein-binding on CL, implies a TDM effect which decreased as dose increases. The model supports a fixed Emax=2.8. Carbamazepine identified as important covariate increasing VPA CL/F by 43%. Clonazepam, Topiramate or Sex were not significant covariates, consistent with previous reports.
raw.structure: 1 compartment model, first-order absorption and elimination
raw.equations: kmax= theta4 k50= theta6 gam1= theta5 kage= (kmax*(AGE**gam1))/((k50**gam1)+(AGE**gam1)) k0=theta3 k1=k0-kage gam2=theta8 TDD50= theta9 TDDmax=theta7 TDD=1+(TDDmax*(DOSE**gam2))/((TDD50**gam2)+(DOSE**gam2)) CL = theta1*(theta2**CBZ)*((WT/70)**k1)*TDD*exp(eta1) V = theta10*(WT/70)*WT if(FORM==1) KA = theta11 #Syrup if(FORM==2) KA = theta12 #Tablets if(FORM==3) KA = theta13 #SR Tablets Cobs=Cipred+eps1 DOSE: VPA dose in mg/day CBZ: comedication with carbamazepine (1: yes, 0: no) WT: total body weight (kg) AGE: age (years) FORM is formulation type, with a value of 1 for syrup, 2 for regular tablets and 3 for slow-release tablets
raw.parameter_values: theta1= 0.300 theta2= 1.43 theta3= 0.791 theta4= 0.096 theta5= 8.63 theta6=0.802 theta7= 2.8 theta8= 1.68 theta9= 37.4 theta10= 22.2 theta11= 2.64 theta12= 1.57 theta13= 0.46 omega1= 0.04752 sigma1=13.3

extraction_target.title: A population pharmacokinetic model of valproic acid in pediatric patients with epilepsy: a non-linear pharmacokinetic model based on protein-binding saturation.

structural_model.description: 1 compartment model, first-order absorption and elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: kmax= theta4 k50= theta6 gam1= theta5 kage= (kmax*(AGE**gam1))/((k50**gam1)+(AGE**gam1)) k0=theta3 k1=k0-kage gam2=theta8 TDD50= theta9 TDDmax=theta7 TDD=1+(TDDmax*(DOSE**gam2))/((TDD50**gam2)+(DOSE**gam2)) CL = theta1*(theta2**CBZ)*((WT/70)**k1)*TDD*exp(eta1) V = theta10*(WT/70)*WT if(FORM==1) KA = theta11 #Syrup if(FORM==2) KA = theta12 #Tablets if(FORM==3) KA = theta13 #SR Tablets Cobs=Cipred+eps1 DOSE: VPA dose in mg/day CBZ: comedication with carbamazepine (1: yes, 0: no) WT: total body weight (kg) AGE: age (years) FORM is formulation type, with a value of 1 for syrup, 2 for regular tablets and 3 for slow-release tablets
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to develop a population pharmacokinetic model for VPA based on protein-binding saturation in pediatric patients with epilepsy.”

covariates.raw: Tested: SEX, AGE, WT, Formulation (Syrup, Conventional tablet or SR tablet), comedication. Included: CBZ, WT, AGE, TDD

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
